data.description: BESSENT ASKED IF COST WOULD EVER MAKE HIM TELL TRUMP WAR NO LONGER AFFORDABLE: "ABSOLUTELY NOT" - SKY NEWS

data.title: Binance secures legal win in Alabama court, with judge ordering plaintiffs to amend complaint or risk dismissal.
data.description: A judge approved a request by Changpeng Zhao, but directed the plaintiffs in the lawsuit against Binance to submit an additional complaint, warning of potential dismissal if they fail to do so.
U.S. TREASURY SECRETARY BESSENT, WHEN ASKED IF RISING COSTS WOULD EVER LEAD HIM TO TELL PRESIDENT TRUMP THE WAR IS NO LONGER AFFORDABLE, FIRMLY RESPONDED "ABSOLUTELY NOT," ACCORDING TO SKY NEWS.

data.title: Solect Energy Details How Rooftop Solar Site Leases Create Long-Term Revenue for Commercial Property Owners
data.description: Property owners in Providence, Rhode Island, can explore alternative revenue streams through rooftop solar site leases offered by Solect Energy. These leases enable commercial and industrial property owners to generate consistent income from their unused rooftop space. By leveraging this overlooked asset, property owners can secure long-term, contractually guaranteed earnings. This initiative presents an opportunity for owners to boost net operating income and optimize asset performance without any upfront investment. Solect Energy's article sheds light on this promising income-generating option for property owners in the region.

data.title: Disrupting Women's Hormonal Health: How Aspect Health is Dismantling PCOS Dogma
data.description: In the healthcare industry, millions of women continue to receive symptom-masking medication instead of receiving comprehensive solutions. Oleg Kovalev, the Chief Marketing Officer at Aspect Health, is shedding light on this prevalent medical issue. Aspect Health has emerged as the leading provider in women's hormonal health solutions, offering a proactive approach to addressing this issue.

data.title: Crude Oil Drops Below $80 After Tanker Escort Claim — Then Rebounds As US Walks It Back (UPDATED)
data.description: Update: Following the announcement by U.S. Department of Energy Secretary Chris Wright that the U.S. Navy safely guided an oil tanker through the Strait of Hormuz, crude oil prices experienced a significant drop. Although Wright's statement about President Trump ensuring energy stability during military actions against Iran was retracted, uncertainty lingers in the energy market. West Texas Intermediate crude, monitored by the United States Oil Fund (NYSE: USO), fell below $80 per barrel in New York by 1:26 p.m., a decrease of almost 33% from the recent peak of $119. This sudden reversal reflects one of the most notable fluctuations in oil markets seen since... (For full story, visit Benzinga.com)

data.title: Maker’s Pride to Close Two Manufacturing Facilities as Part of Strategic Network Optimization
data.description: Maker’s Pride has recently revealed plans to gradually cease operations at its manufacturing plants in Salt Lake City, Utah, and Shakopee, Minnesota. The closures are slated to finalize in the third quarter of 2026. This move comes after an extensive evaluation of the company’s manufacturing infrastructure and future expansion plans. Maker’s Pride aims to focus on key growth areas and enhance operational efficiency through the incorporation of advanced automation technologies.

data.title: Ark Invest Warns 35% Of Bitcoin Supply Could Face Future Quantum Risks, But There Is Time To Adapt
data.description: A recent report by Ark Invest and Unchained suggests that advancements in quantum computing could potentially pose a challenge to Bitcoin's security measures in the future. The report, titled "Bitcoin and Quantum Computing," highlights that current quantum machines are still not sufficiently powerful to breach Bitcoin's security. Presently, these systems are operating in the Noisy Intermediate-Scale Quantum (NISQ) era, which means they have limited computational capabilities and high error rates, thus preventing them from compromising Bitcoin's cryptographic defenses. Bitcoin's security is primarily upheld by cryptographic hash functions and elliptic curve cryptography (ECC). Although the potential threat from quantum computing looms, it remains distant for the time being.

data.title: HelloNation and U.S. First Responders Association Announce New Partnership to Spotlight First Responder Service and Community Impact
data.description: In Panama City, Florida on March 12, 2026, a partnership was announced to highlight the efforts of first responders nationwide. This initiative aims to honor the work of first responders and shed light on their valuable contributions.

data.title: Consumer Watchdog: 5 Ways To Lower California Gasoline Prices, Which Are $2 More Than US Prices In Bay Area
data.description: In Los Angeles on March 12, 2026, Jamie Court, President of Consumer Watchdog, addressed the surge in California gasoline prices. Court attributed the spike to the conflict in Iran, which has caused a hike in crude oil prices. However, Court indicated that this is just one factor contributing to the state's average price of $5 per gallon.

data.title: In HelloNation, Home Improvement Expert Logan MacMillen Explains How Visual & Functional Upgrades Refresh a Home
